239363.explanation: The SGP is cautious about raising taxes, so this statement fits that stance. However, sufficient reserves should remain to absorb future setbacks.

239364.explanation: Tourist tax is not only intended for nature areas; it can also be used for facilities in our beautiful agricultural countryside, such as a bicycle path.

239365.explanation: Abolishing the dog tax is the SGP ideal, although we must first find coverage for the costs made for, for example, dog walking fields. Dog owners in rural areas hardly use these facilities, which already indicates that this tax is less fair.

239366.explanation: The municipality of Ede has so many qualities that it sells itself. The money can be better spent.

239367.explanation: Sunday rest is a gift from God; it is good to take a day for family, church attendance and rest. The SGP therefore does not support the idea of a 24-hour economy.

239368.explanation: Our agricultural lands are desperately needed for producing tasty food. These fertile lands are also very scarce. So we must prevent their conversion into nature as much as possible. Besides, the municipality of Ede already consists of more than half nature!

239369.explanation: Asylum seekers who, after often lengthy court procedures, have been told by the judge to leave the Netherlands must comply. The SGP does not support resistance against these judicial decisions.

239370.explanation: The SGP wants every (rental) housing seeker with urgency to be treated equally. For example, a mother urgently seeking housing due to domestic violence. There is a committee that assesses urgency cases; they can weigh who has the greatest urgency.

239371.explanation: According to the SGP, solar panels belong on the roof! Our fertile agricultural land is far too scarce to place solar panels there. Many roofs still lack panels; the SGP focuses on that. This is also a much smarter use of our scarce space.

239372.explanation: The SGP supports sufficient public toilets. However, we first want to see proof that there are currently too few before building more. We handle tax money carefully, money paid by you.

239373.explanation: Research shows that residents of the Municipality of Ede are quite satisfied with the cultural offerings. The SGP believes that fewer festivals and events could be organized. This might also reduce the costs often incurred by the municipality for them.

239374.explanation: For a better sense of security, a larger number of enforcers can help. We are happy to work with our mayor to determine where and what expansion is desired.

239375.explanation: The SGP would like Ede to be free of coffeeshops. Drugs are harmful, and coffeeshops often stimulate addictions.

239376.explanation: If it turns out that fat bike riders commit violations more often, the SGP agrees stricter enforcement is justified.

239377.explanation: To keep the starter loan effective, it would be good if the threshold were raised—for example, to the NHG limit. This would also automatically index it annually. (adjusted)

239378.explanation: There is a large shortage of social rental housing. To prevent our own residents from having to wait even longer, they should be given priority over house seekers from outside the municipality of Ede.

239379.explanation: Because of the large shortage of social rental housing, building many seems appealing. Many tenants would rather buy a home. Therefore, the SGP also focuses on additional social owner-occupied housing.

239380.explanation: To address the current housing shortage, building upwards is almost inevitable. But only in specific locations suited for it, such as in Ede-Center.

239381.explanation: Ede already has very generous arrangements for those less well off. The SGP is in favor of helping residents in poverty; we want to support them out of Biblical compassion. Expanding these arrangements to more residents is not necessary. We prefer to help those who really need it as best as possible.

239382.explanation: Cancellation of municipal taxes for a family that just barely gets by makes a big difference. The SGP therefore does not want to stop this arrangement out of Biblical compassion.

239383.explanation: Research shows that car traffic will continue to grow in the future. Additionally, the municipality of Ede is also growing, so more, not fewer, parking spaces are needed. Car traffic is also becoming 'greener,' so the SGP does not find it necessary to fight against cars.

239385.explanation: On some roads within built-up areas, 30 km/h is a safe speed. It also benefits cyclists. On roads used as routes for our emergency services, 30 km/h is not advisable. Also, in rural areas, 30 km/h is often not necessary.

239386.explanation: If you experience discrimination, you can report it via the discrimination reporting point. The SGP finds this well arranged; appointing an additional coordinator is not necessary. These costs would have to be paid from your taxes.

239387.explanation: The SGP has no signals that this is necessary. There is already a staff shortage, and all hours spent on community service could have been used for, for example, issuing your permit.

239389.explanation: The SGP opposes any further expansion. Also if the current plans prove unfeasible.

239391.explanation: The SGP finds it unfair to let only new residents benefit from free membership. Moreover, 'free' sounds nice, but the bill still needs to be paid. Is that with your tax money?

239392.explanation: Research shows that residents of our municipality are quite satisfied with the supply of art and culture. The SGP believes we can see where it could be less or more self-reliant. Much beauty is created without community funds.

239393.explanation: The rainbow is a sign of God's goodness and faithfulness; the SGP therefore does not consider it a suitable symbol for diversity policy.

239394.explanation: The SGP believes we should listen not only to proponents of more events but especially to residents. In this case, neighbors bear the nuisance. Therefore, the SGP opposes this expansion.

239397.explanation: Clarity towards residents increases support. The SGP therefore advocates clear agreements.

239398.explanation: Accommodating the currently legally required number of asylum seekers already requires much creativity. The municipality also has responsibilities towards its own residents, especially considering long waiting lists for (rental) housing.

239399.explanation: Accommodation locations should be where they fit; this can also be on the edge of a town. Taking into account the support of local residents.

239400.explanation: Dutch farmers are among the most environmentally and animal-friendly farmers in the world. Reducing livestock farming here often means increasing it elsewhere in the world with much less attention to environment and animals. Moreover, livestock farming is already shrinking, mainly due to government intervention.

239401.explanation: A strong and well-trained military is very important in these times of many tensions between countries. Our safety comes first, and training is part of that. The SGP also believes residents should be well informed and involved.

239402.explanation: Sometimes it is unavoidable because the municipality has other tasks as well. Think of preventing heat stress in residential areas and taking care of cables and pipes under the parking spaces. The SGP believes there must be enough parking spaces.

239403.explanation: The province is the competent authority for matters regarding wolf management. The SGP supports wolf-free zones in our municipality; it is good if the province is urged to implement effective measures against nuisance, for example, when sheep or ponies are attacked on farms.

239404.explanation: More than half of the municipality of Ede consists of nature. Many nature areas are already closed off for recreation; according to the SGP, no more areas need to be closed off. Nature is also meant to be enjoyed together!

239405.explanation: There must be enough space to detain 'criminals'. If it turns out that returning the cell complex in Ede is necessary for this, the SGP supports the return of the cell complex in Ede.

239406.explanation: A camera on the uniform of BOAs regularly prevents situations from getting out of hand. People behave more controlled towards the BOA because they realize everything is being recorded. Therefore, the SGP is in favor of these cameras. Also for your safety!

239407.explanation: Many crimes have been solved thanks to the use of a police dog. A dog's sense of smell is many times stronger than that of the best police officer. For these reasons alone, the SGP appreciates our police dogs.

239408.explanation: This is not a municipal task. However, your municipality must ensure a public space that is as safe as possible. If safety matters to you, the SGP is the right address.

239409.explanation: The SGP supports building as much as possible to meet the needs of our own residents. For the villages, this means small-scale construction projects tailored to the wishes that are present in the villages. Large-scale projects often attract much interest from, for example, the Randstad, which the SGP considers undesirable.

239410.explanation: The municipality exists to stimulate and partly enable housing construction. The SGP does not think the municipality should build itself; there are sufficient construction companies and developers for that.

239412.explanation: This seems like a sympathetic plan but it is not. The SGP wants the municipality to engage in dialogue with those owners. What is the reason? Can the municipality do anything about it? For example, sometimes a permit is awaited, or a construction company does not have time yet. Solve problems instead of creating burdens!

239413.explanation: There is a very large shortage of houses in Ede. Therefore, we do not rule out that high-rise buildings are necessary. For example, these also fit well in parts of Veldhuizen. The SGP is critical: the building must fit with the rest of the environment.

239415.explanation: Sports cost money; nothing is free. If the municipality starts paying for this, it will have to cut somewhere else or raise taxes. Can you really not afford it? The municipality already has generous schemes to help you. Feel free to inquire with the municipality.

239416.explanation: Paying for the veterinarian is not a municipal task. The SGP understands you want the best for your pet. Fortunately, the municipality does have other arrangements if you have a low income. For example, think of 'Ede doet mee!'

239417.explanation: The SGP wishes every child a good and healthy breakfast so the child has energy for the new day! Paying for breakfast for all children goes too far for us and is also unnecessary. Tailored solutions for the parents and children who truly cannot afford it seem better.

239418.explanation: For many entrepreneurs, electric transport is not yet practical or financially feasible. The SGP supports clean air! Experience also shows that an environmental zone in one city means transporters move their diesel trucks elsewhere. Overall, this makes little difference.

239419.explanation: The municipality should not do this but may do so. It also relates to the applicable urgency policy: who has the most urgency for that home? Sometimes shared housing is an option, and the SGP has called for national attention for hospitability rental and alternative housing forms for this target group.

239420.explanation: The elderly deserve our attention. Home visits can sometimes reduce or prevent loneliness.

239421.explanation: Family coaches have a valuable task: supporting families in times of difficulty. If it turns out more money is needed, the SGP is willing to provide it. If there are other things that can be improved, then it is good to work on that!

239363.explanation: The municipality must handle residents' money carefully. According to ChristenUnie Ede, the municipality needs sufficient funds for facilities such as community centers, sports fields, bike paths, and libraries. In our opinion, expenditures must be well substantiated.

239364.explanation: Besides costs for maintaining nature areas, other costs related to tourism are also incurred. Nature areas must be well maintained, but this does not necessarily have to be paid from the tourist tax.

239365.explanation: More and more municipalities are abolishing the dog tax. This can also happen in Ede as far as we are concerned, as long as facilities such as dog toilets and dog exercise fields are not affected and enforcement on cleaning up dog poop is maintained. That means we free up funds for this in another way.

239366.explanation: We find it important that there is sufficient space for local economic activity. We also want room for circular business parks and places for starting, innovative entrepreneurs. There must be short lines of communication between the municipality and entrepreneurs.

239367.explanation: ChristenUnie Ede believes in the usefulness of a collective day of rest. Entrepreneurs and their retail staff also need moments of rest and relaxation. This means that opportunities for Sunday openings will not be further expanded and shopkeepers will not be obliged to open on shopping Sundays.

239368.explanation: A long-term vision is needed to clarify where space is offered for, for example, water storage and new nature. This is necessary to offer perspective to permanent farmers. Trust and cooperation are important in this. The municipality plays an important role in this.

239369.explanation: Even rejected asylum seekers need shelter. There must remain prospects for deportation. By providing shelter, we keep them in sight and reduce the risk of exploitation or crime. We prefer that shelter to be organized regionally.

239370.explanation: Priority ensures a good distribution of status holders across the municipality. If we stop this, we cannot fulfill our municipal duty and asylum centers will overflow with all the consequences that entails. We want to build many more social rental homes so the waiting time decreases for everyone.

239371.explanation: ChristenUnie Ede favors other applications of solar panels, for example on roofs and above parking spaces. New (large-scale) solar fields on agricultural land are not our preference.

239372.explanation: Sufficient public toilets must be available in shopping areas. If this is not possible with municipal facilities, we make agreements with entrepreneurs, retailers, and such to open their toilets. Signage must be adequate so public toilets are easy to find.

239373.explanation: A growing municipality also brings a greater need for events. We must take this into account in plans, such as the Development Framework Ede-Stad. There must always be room for events in the villages, such as De Valkse Dag, the Old Lunterse Day, and the Vlegeldag.

239374.explanation: We see that the safety of our residents and safety issues demand increasing attention. More enforcers can help, although we prefer people to look out for each other. In neighborhoods and streets, in churches and clubs, at schools and sports fields. That is the foundation for real safety.

239375.explanation: We see that substance use can sometimes have disastrous consequences for health, relationships, and society. Cooperation with social partners and youth work is important to counteract this. Not renewing permits can contribute to reducing drug use.

239376.explanation: We see that unsafe situations can arise on bike paths due to speed differences between cyclists. This is partly caused by fatbikes. The municipality can ask the police to pay extra attention to this. However, the police themselves prioritize their work.

239377.explanation: For starters, the search for a first home can feel hopeless. There is high demand and relatively little supply. This results in increasingly high house prices. In this tight market, starters must still be able to participate seriously.

239378.explanation: We find it important that both elderly and youth can live in places with which they already have ties. We see there is a particularly strong need for this in the villages.

239379.explanation: Not only in Ede but also in the villages, there is much demand for social rental housing. We have been calling attention to this for some time. For us, 30% social rental in new construction plans is a minimum, and where possible, we add a bit more. It is important that neighborhoods remain mixed.

239380.explanation: The housing shortage is so great that high-rise buildings are necessary. We also want to preserve as much nature and farmland as possible with this choice. The residential towers must, however, be beautifully designed with many spaces for (shared) green outdoor areas and social interaction.

239381.explanation: We want to keep the existing schemes. Currently, they are used by too few people. That is unfortunate because the schemes do help. We want a simple City Pass where it is immediately clear which schemes people with a low income qualify for.

239382.explanation: ChristenUnie Ede wants to retain the possibility of exemption from municipal taxes for residents with a low income. In fact, more attention to this possibility is welcome.

239383.explanation: Existing parking spaces do not need to disappear, but for new developments, we apply, where possible, a lower parking standard.

239385.explanation: It is not safe when cars drive 50 km/h on roads without separated bike paths. Within neighborhoods, ChristenUnie Ede prefers 30 km/h. In streets without sidewalks, such as home zones, this is 15 km/h. We focus on a number of good arterial roads where 50 km/h is allowed.

239386.explanation: Discrimination against diverse groups and individuals is a persistent problem. We will need to monitor where this occurs in some way so that we can intervene where possible.

239387.explanation: The idea sounds good, but when we weigh the benefits of such an internship against the costs and the declining productivity, we have our doubts. However, we do want to continue working with a social service time for young people, as society indeed benefits from this.

239389.explanation: It is good that the Frisokazerne is being repurposed. Akoesticum and Redrum are strong parties that have already proven themselves. Museum 'De Basis' still needs to prove itself. For now, we have sufficient confidence that these three participants can strengthen each other.

239391.explanation: As ChristenUnie, we want all residents of Ede to be able to participate, for example through a broad City Pass and the Sports Card. In our view, this should apply not only to new residents but certainly also to current ones. People on minimum income should naturally continue to be eligible for support schemes.

239392.explanation: The municipality of Ede invests relatively little money in arts and culture. From the ChristenUnie Ede perspective, no additional funding is necessary, but cutting the arts and culture budget is not a good idea for Ede as a growing municipality.

239393.explanation: We find it important to keep the conversation going about inclusivity because as ChristenUnie we stand for a safe environment for everyone. Everyone should be able to be themselves. As far as we are concerned, sports clubs should make this choice themselves, since a requirement could work counterproductively.

239394.explanation: At the moment, expanding the number of large events is not advisable. Before expansion is up for discussion, trust needs to be restored among local residents, enforcement against disturbances must improve, and different types of events should get a chance.

239397.explanation: We believe in small-scale, permanent shelter locations. Setting up and dismantling locations takes a lot of time and money. It pays off to invest in locations that are open for a long time, which improves the quality of the shelter.

239398.explanation: The numbers from the dispersion law guide us. If there is lower inflow for a number of years, we want to keep shelters open and retain the experience gained and the commitment of volunteers. We want to use available rooms for asylum seekers, urgent seekers, and students.

239399.explanation: We want asylum seekers and people with residence permits to fully participate in our society and enable residents to easily establish contact. This way, integration can take much better shape. Good accessibility of shelter locations (via public transport) also contributes to this.

239400.explanation: Reduction as a goal in itself is too simplistic, but it is necessary to work towards fewer (intensive) livestock farms and to transition to other forms of agriculture. We want quick perspectives for sustainable farmers, where trust and collaboration are important.

239401.explanation: We will all notice it: more exercises in our municipality with occasional disturbances or increased risk of incidents (such as wildfires). Freedom must cost us something, and since Ede has suitable training locations, Defense may exercise here according to us.

239402.explanation: In built-up neighborhoods with sufficient parking, greening is a good idea. This contributes to a pleasant and healthy living environment.

239403.explanation: Together with the province and the national government, we focus on active management. Wolf-proof fences should be encouraged, and in our view, problematic wolves should be allowed to be culled. The wolf has a place in nature reserves. For the ecosystem, it is an added value.

239404.explanation: Residents and tourists should be able to enjoy the beauty that our surroundings have to offer. We want to focus on a good distribution of accessible and protected nature, with extra attention from both the municipality and visitors to keeping nature areas clean.

239405.explanation: Having our own detention facility has added value: less dependency on other municipalities and the ability to act quickly where necessary. From our viewpoint, the municipality should pay attention to this. Ultimately, the decision rests with the police on whether to (re)open a detention facility in Ede.

239406.explanation: Enforcement officers have limited equipment. A small camera can have a preventive effect and properly record what happens in difficult situations. In this case, we prioritize safety over the risk of reduced privacy.

239407.explanation: Police dogs are specially trained for their task. For example, they are experts in searching for missing persons and prohibited substances. They also play an important role in disturbances of public order. We are happy with these strong and smart four-legged friends!

239408.explanation: Every woman should feel free and safe, both at home and on the street. That is why we invest in prevention and awareness. Our goal is that every woman can live without fear and has all the space to develop her talents.

239409.explanation: There is a great need for housing in villages as well. When building new homes, villages are explicitly our focus. The development must fit the identity of the village and local demand. We want to build according to need and particularly stimulate housing circulation within the villages themselves.

239410.explanation: We can stimulate housing construction for affordable homes that the market does not build sufficiently. The municipality can help, for example, with lower land prices or a housing fund. The municipality can also work more on plans where social housing is part of it.

239412.explanation: Vacancy must be prevented. And if there is vacancy, action must be taken. If a financial incentive helps, then we certainly want to impose that tax. Research into its effectiveness for the Ede situation is a prerequisite.

239413.explanation: There are several places in Ede where height accents can be added. We cannot afford to limit ourselves to just these two areas. Of course, it must be examined carefully where high-rise buildings are appropriate and where not.

239415.explanation: Sports should be low-threshold and accessible for all young people in Ede. That is why we want the Ede Sports Card to be available for all schools and students, so that every young person can flexibly get to know a broad range of sports. Sportservice can play a coordinating role in this.

239416.explanation: Veterinary care is expensive. We admit that immediately. However, we do not believe it is the municipality's task to reimburse medical costs for animals. There are foundations and funds that can help.

239417.explanation: A good, healthy breakfast is especially important for children. Academic performance declines if there is hunger. We want equal opportunities for every child. Schools are places where poverty is identified and where solutions can also be offered.

239418.explanation: The start date has already been postponed once. It is now important to create clarity for entrepreneurs, as it is difficult to get business operations in order if the start date keeps shifting. In addition, we continue to work for a healthy living environment.

239419.explanation: For some homeless people, an urgency declaration or 'Housing First' is helpful. Then they can be helped to housing quickly. Other homeless people with additional problems sometimes benefit more from other types of shelter such as 'Alternative Living'.

239420.explanation: Older people remain living independently longer. Timely discussions about possible support needs are desirable. Home visits offer that opportunity. We lower the visit age from 80 to 75 years. This way, we ensure earlier contact, better detection, and timely appropriate support.

239421.explanation: We believe that parents should be able to quickly and clearly find their way to appropriate support. Together with professionals, we want to build policy that prevents families from unnecessarily getting stuck, because that limits human suffering and saves healthcare costs.

239363.explanation: Lowering the property tax to give back reserves may seem attractive, but reserves are meant for stability. Not as a piggy bank to be emptied.

239364.explanation: The municipal costs of maintaining our tourist areas cover more than just nature reserves. It also includes waste disposal, enforcement, maintaining infrastructure, and public facilities.

239365.explanation: Since more and more municipalities are abolishing this, we want to make this a spearhead for the coming period. Especially because this money goes to the general reserves and is not necessarily spent on the dog.

239366.explanation: The central location of the municipality of Ede ensures that we are already an attractive municipality for businesses.

239367.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes this should be the choice of entrepreneurs.

239368.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that our agricultural land must remain protected. Farmers in our municipality should be able to continue farming.

239369.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that every person who is illegally in the Netherlands should leave the country as soon as possible.

239370.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that giving priority to one specific group of people creates inequality. Moreover, it prolongs waiting times for other housing seekers.

239371.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that our agricultural land must be protected. Let's first look at other locations such as large roofs of companies and only then at agricultural land.

239372.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede has previously indicated that many more public toilets should be installed in our village centers.

239373.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that festivals and events have many positive effects on our economy, social cohesion, and culture.

239374.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede considers a pleasant living environment essential. More enforcement officers on the streets can prevent nuisance or quickly nip it in the bud. Moreover, the visibility of enforcement officers increases the feeling of safety.

239375.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede is of the opinion that closing coffee shops causes street deals and nuisance. Extending the licenses contributes to less illegal trade and is easier to control.

239376.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that fatbikes cause a lot of nuisance especially in Ede center and in other shopping and pedestrian areas.

239377.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds the limit of €355,000 too low for the current housing market. Raising this limit makes buying more realistic, fairer, and financially feasible for first-time buyers.

239378.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that giving priority to home seekers from our own villages contributes to livability and a strong local community.

239379.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels that a mandatory 40% social housing can delay the construction of homes, limit diversity in the neighborhood, and insufficiently take current market demand into account.

239380.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes these residential towers provide more homes, more efficient use of space, affordable housing, and sustainable urban growth without unnecessarily using a lot of land.

239381.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede holds that the aim of poverty schemes is to support the most vulnerable, as has been done in recent years.

239382.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that the municipality can provide better targeted support to the most vulnerable through allowances, debt counseling, or food assistance.

239383.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that reducing parking spaces decreases accessibility. It also affects livability and economic activity in our municipality.

239385.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede considers this ineffective. Targeted customization ensures more safety and better flow.

239386.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes a separate coordinator is unnecessary. Effective monitoring and combating discrimination is done through existing structures.

239387.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede thinks it is costly and places a strain on staffing.

239389.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes this location is suitable.

239391.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels that free membership is unfair to current members.

239392.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that art and culture create connection and livability. Moreover, they make our municipality attractive.

239393.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes sport is for everyone, without political or social stances.

239394.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that partly due to our municipality’s growth, there is more need for events.

239397.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that an agreed date provides clarity to local residents. A deal is a deal.

239398.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels our municipality already provides many places. More locations put pressure on local facilities and livability.

239399.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that this negatively affects the livability, safety, and privacy of residents.

239400.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede thinks our livestock farms are important for the local economy.

239401.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes the exercises are important for our safety and to be well prepared for possible conflict situations.

239402.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels that otherwise too few parking spaces remain for residents, visitors, and businesses.

239403.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes the wolf should be managed like all other wildlife.

239404.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede thinks people should be free to enjoy and relax in our beautiful nature.

239405.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that having a separate cell complex is much more efficient and less expensive.

239406.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes these cameras contribute to safety and gathering evidence.

239407.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels that dogs help contain major unrest and track down offenders.

239408.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede considers this a personal choice for each individual, but at their own expense.

239409.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes we have a severe housing shortage that must be resolved.

239410.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede holds that this should not be provided by the municipality itself. There are appropriate partners for that.

239412.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that a person owning a long-term vacant building should not be penalized by charging taxes.

239413.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that high-rise buildings require customization. They must fit into the environment.

239415.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes everyone should be able to play sports. A special arrangement already exists for the less well-off.

239416.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that people who choose to acquire a pet must also be able to carry the associated costs.

239417.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds that schools, for children who do not get breakfast at home, can make use of the existing arrangement that enables breakfast at school.

239418.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes this target date is not achievable. Especially adapting the entrepreneurs' vehicle fleets takes time.

239419.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede feels that necessary help and guidance must first be looked at before independent living becomes an option.

239420.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede finds the current approach by, among others, Malkander appropriate. Visiting elderly people and asking if there is any need.

239421.explanation: GemeenteBelangen Ede believes that the deployment of family coaches can contribute preventively in a more complex assistance trajectory.

239363.explanation: We can use saved reserves for many plans to make the municipality greener and more social. The property tax in Ede is already lower than average.

239364.explanation: We want to use the tourist tax mainly for the maintenance of nature reserves, as they are important and under most pressure from tourists. Exceptions can be made in rare cases.

239365.explanation: Dogs have long ceased to be working animals, and other pets, such as cats, also leave droppings that need to be cleaned up.

239366.explanation: Ede is already an attractive place to settle; we do not need to encourage attracting even more companies to this area.

239367.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports the freedom of choice for people to arrange their lives as they wish. We want freedom of choice for entrepreneurs and residents, as long as employees are not forced. Solidarity with workers is paramount.

239368.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to combine the agricultural transition with the restoration of nature in our municipality. This offers a favorable long-term perspective for our farmers and our nature. We support extensification of agriculture and agricultural nature management. Young and sustainable farmers should be able to take over from those who stop. Parts of agricultural land may be used for nature reserves.

239369.explanation: Human dignity stands above migration status. Basic shelter is a human right, irrespective of residence permit. Moreover, these people often fall through the cracks and the alternative is that they end up sleeping on the street.

239370.explanation: Refugees with residence permits have the right to housing and must be able to integrate quickly. Priority prevents prolonged stay in shelters and promotes participation in society. The housing crisis results from years of failing housing policies and market forces, not from refugees.

239371.explanation: We are working towards a comprehensive sustainable energy mix for Ede, including solar, wind, heat reuse, and geothermal where possible. We are exploring where solar fields can be added, such as on farmland with farmers who want this.

239372.explanation: GL-PvdA wants everyone to participate. Due to lack of public toilets, some have to stay home more often than they would like. These are often elderly people and those with medical conditions. We advocate for better toilet facilities so they too can go out.

239373.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA Ede wants more money for culture. We invest more in libraries, performing arts, live art, a music venue, and community centers. Both in Ede and its villages, we want everyone to enjoy festivals and events.

239374.explanation: Bouncers (BOAs) contribute to livability and safety, mainly through prevention, addressing issues, and presence. The focus should be on social behavior and problem prevention. Additional funds for safety we want to allocate to women’s shelters, which are lacking in Ede.

239375.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes coffeeshops are allowed as long as they respect the neighborhood and comply with good regulations.

239376.explanation: The police enforce all traffic violations. The police can decide themselves when more controls are needed without municipal interference. We also do not want to unnecessarily target specific groups.

239377.explanation: The starter loan is a good tool to enable more young people to buy a home. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to offer this structurally. The scheme must not have a price-inflation effect, so we must keep a close watch on that.

239378.explanation: There are priority schemes for people from the municipality of Ede. GroenLinks-PvdA supports this but also wants to give opportunities to people coming from outside to live here. We do not want to pit groups against each other; the priority is to provide sufficient affordable and good housing.

239379.explanation: The target is 30% social rental housing in every municipality. Ede is far behind. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants all new construction projects to consist of at least 40% social rental housing. Furthermore, 40% should be mid-rent and mid-buy. In the coming four years, we will add at least 4,000 homes in Ede and its centers.

239380.explanation: In specific areas, for example around the Ede-Wageningen station, we find this appropriate. To fit with the surroundings, we do not want to build much higher than the current high-rise in many places. We want sustainable building in Ede, with reusable materials and low energy costs.

239381.explanation: Existential security is a core value. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers prevention over repression; daily stress and uncertainty about making ends meet increase the risk of illness, debts, and homelessness. GroenLinks-PvdA wants all children to grow up carefree and all residents of Ede to make ends meet well.

239382.explanation: We are investigating whether Ede can offer debt forgiveness for problematic debts to families with children. We do not want children to grow up in poverty. The costs pay off: homelessness is much more expensive for society.

239383.explanation: We encourage the use of shared cars. We want to make cycling, walking, and public transport more attractive. We review parking policy in conjunction with urban design, accessibility, and mobility. This means fewer parking spaces are needed, but that is a consequence, not an end in itself.

239385.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA improves road safety: on all roads without a separate cycle path, the speed limit will be 30 km/h. On roads where 30 km/h is not appropriate, we will ensure better separation between cars and cyclists.

239386.explanation: Structural inequality requires structural policy. This can be directed and supervised by an anti-discrimination coordinator. This will lead to anti-discrimination provisions, such as reporting points and education in schools. Our municipal policy will actively combat discrimination and exclusion, from the labor market to the housing market.

239387.explanation: Civil servants are already there for the public interest. A mandatory social internship does not automatically contribute to better service. Engagement with society is important, but that works better through voluntary initiatives and room for own effort.

239389.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give local makers room in developing the new museum concept. We also want the cultural center to be accessible to everyone and appeal to diverse target groups.

239391.explanation: This is good for social cohesion and participation. Moreover, we want to lower financial barriers for adults and families with minimum incomes so that everyone can play sports.

239392.explanation: Ede already spends below average on culture. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA wants to increase the budget for culture in our growing municipality. Culture is not a side issue but social infrastructure.

239393.explanation: This aligns with the Ede sports agreement: sport is for everyone. Therefore, we call on sports clubs to fly the Progress Pride flag during, among others, Pride, Coming Out Day, IDAHOT, and Purple Friday.

239394.explanation: A large event means a lot of noise nuisance for the neighborhood. Therefore, we prefer to expand the Open Air Theatre, which is located in the middle of a residential area, with beautiful performances that cause less disturbance. This way, everyone can enjoy it: the visitors and the neighbors.

239397.explanation: This creates uncertainty and hampers integration. People are not temporary projects. Clear agreements with the neighborhood are always important, but an end date cannot be promised.

239398.explanation: Solidarity is not a minimum but a moral choice. Currently, Ede is actually below its sheltering quota. There is a lot of unrest worldwide, and all municipalities in the Netherlands, including Ede, must show moral leadership.

239399.explanation: We want at least two permanent shelter locations with a maximum of 250 places for refugees. GroenLinks-PvdA believes these should be situated in the heart of society so new residents can actively integrate and participate. We do this in good consultation with the neighborhood.

239400.explanation: Less livestock means less emission of greenhouse gases and nitrogen. Much meat is destined for export, whereas the pollution stays with us. Industrial livestock farming is harmful to the climate, nature, health, and animal welfare. We want an animal welfare policy to better treat animals.

239401.explanation: Ede is already doing a lot in this area and since 2024 the expansion of defense in Ede has increased significantly. A large part of military training grounds in our municipality lies in a Natura 2000 area. Therefore, we prefer not to expand further in this area to protect our unique nature.

239402.explanation: We want to green parking spaces and squares in existing neighborhoods with water-permeable and green paving. On hot days, this greatly reduces heat stress. By promoting shared cars and other traffic forms, not all parking spaces will be needed anymore.

239403.explanation: We support and encourage wolf deterrent measures and solutions for livestock farmers and hobby farmers. Education about how to deal with wilder nature removes unnecessary worries among residents. The wolf is important for nature restoration and enhances biodiversity.

239404.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to view nature only in terms of its utility for humans. This may mean that quiet areas for animals need to be adjusted. We are careful and proud of the special nature we have, which is why we believe humans must adapt to it.

239405.explanation: More repression does not solve social problems. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to focus on early prevention, for example through education and the presence of good (youth) care services in all neighborhoods.

239406.explanation: Both the enforcement officers (boa's) and the people they act against are protected from improper violence. However, the footage must be handled very carefully, and nothing should be unnecessarily retained. Strict compliance with privacy regulations is essential.

239407.explanation: Police dogs may be used for tasks such as locating missing children. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to stop using police dogs at demonstrations; this is disproportionate and increases the risk of violence. We also want to limit the use of dogs for animal welfare reasons.

239408.explanation: Emancipation and safety are public responsibilities. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to focus on measures that do not place the responsibility on women themselves, but offering free training fits within a broader approach.

239409.explanation: The villages can and want to contribute to our housing task. We always do this in a way that suits the environment. We will start as soon as possible on the plan from the Lunteren Village Council for the ‘Westzoom Lunteren,’ which allows for 1,200 homes. We always want to base it on 40% social housing.

239410.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to investigate the possibilities for a municipal construction company. Public housing is a government task and the market is failing: then the municipality must take the lead again on what, where and how is built.

239412.explanation: In times of housing crisis, vacancy is unacceptable. If a building stands empty for more than a year, the municipality may levy a tax to encourage good use of all space so that as many people as possible can find suitable housing.

239413.explanation: This depends on how tall the high-rise buildings are. GroenLinks-PvdA always wants to take the surroundings into account when building. High-rise buildings are most logical around the station area and in the center of Ede city.

239415.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants everyone to be able to play sports. We want to encourage young people to start sports. The Ede sports card is a good instrument for this. By making this free for young people, we make healthy exercise accessible.

239416.explanation: A pet is a support and socially very important for many people, especially when they are struggling otherwise. From a social point of view and animal welfare, it is good to help out when really needed.

239417.explanation: More and more children have a poor or no breakfast, often due to poverty. This leads to poor concentration and less energy and thus less equal opportunities. A free breakfast at school, consistent with the Dutch healthy eating guidelines, can be a low-threshold solution.

239418.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes further delay of measures against pollution. This schedule provides sufficient time for entrepreneurs to take measures and for the municipality to facilitate this.

239419.explanation: Housing First is a proven method to combat homelessness. It is often cheaper than alternatives and provides people with a stable base from which they can rebuild their lives. Together with care services, life is put back on track.

239420.explanation: Welfare workers can add a lot, also for people aged 75 and older, when they need it themselves.

239421.explanation: Difficult situations can sometimes feel hopeless for families. A family coach can help to address issues quickly and effectively, thereby preventing stress, unnecessary deterioration, and even higher costs.

239363.explanation: The municipality must be careful with the money it collects from the people of Ede. If it collects too much, taxes can be reduced. However, the municipality must also carefully consider important matters such as housing, economy, sports, and culture which require funding.

239364.explanation: We all enjoy our beautiful nature. Therefore, the income from tourism must be used to preserve it. At the same time, we must invest in villages (such as Otterlo) that bear a large part of the tourism impact.

239365.explanation: Everyone loves their four-legged friend! At the same time, cleaning up dog poop costs the municipality a lot of money. Therefore, it is only fair that dog owners contribute to these costs.

239366.explanation: Businesses need a favorable business climate: good locations, sufficient network capacity, and skilled professionals ready to work. That is why we want the municipality to invest in these aspects and not in advertising.

239367.explanation: Freedom is very important. Everyone should be able to shape their own form of Sunday rest. That is why we want entrepreneurs to have the freedom to choose whether or not to open, including on Sundays.

239368.explanation: Farmers deserve a clear perspective. For this, it is important that nitrogen emissions decrease. Nature and agriculture should go hand in hand as much as possible. We see beautiful examples of this in the Binnenveld area.

239369.explanation: Asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands must return to their country of origin. That is where the government must first focus. Until then, no one will be left to sleep on the street.

239370.explanation: We want to build so much that prioritization is no longer needed to house enough people. Additionally, Ede must fulfill its housing obligations. This does not mean that priority is automatically given.

239371.explanation: Farmers and landowners must be free to choose to use their land for generating sustainable energy. However, we prefer solar panels to be placed on roofs.

239372.explanation: D66 wants more public toilets because this increases accessibility and inclusivity in the city, especially for the elderly, people with disabilities, and women. It contributes to hygiene, freedom of movement, and a welcoming, livable public space.

239373.explanation: D66 Ede believes that festivals and events are important for culture, economy, and a vibrant municipality. Events like Heideweek, Vlegeldag and Oud-Lunterse Dag attract many visitors. According to D66 Ede, there is room for more, but not at any cost and with attention to nuisance.

239374.explanation: Everyone in Ede must feel safe on the streets. Young or old, male or female, everyone should be able to enjoy a safe Ede. That is why we want enforcement officers (BOAs) to also focus on safety. This way, there are more eyes keeping everyone safe.

239375.explanation: Everyone is free to decide whether they want to use a coffeeshop. The municipality should not further restrict that freedom. Banning coffeeshops would lead to more illegal dealers and nuisance.

239376.explanation: Many people experience nuisance from people on fat bikes, for example in Ede's shopping street. The municipality can discuss this with the police, but ultimately the police know best how to deploy their limited time.

239377.explanation: Starters have great difficulty finding an affordable home. The average starter home costs about €385,000. Therefore, we want to raise the maximum price of a home eligible for a starter loan to €385,000.

239378.explanation: The average waiting time for social rental housing is 4 years and increases every year. Therefore, we need to build more affordable housing. People with ties to our beautiful municipality should be given priority.

239379.explanation: Residents of Ede currently wait an average of 4 years on the waiting list for social rental housing. To make real progress, we want to aim for 40% social housing wherever possible, unless it endangers housing construction. This way, we reduce waiting lists and build for everyone.

239380.explanation: On average, you have to wait 4 years in Ede for social rental housing. This waiting time gets longer every year. Therefore, D66 Ede wants to build somewhat higher in certain locations. This prevents building in green areas and ensures affordable homes for everyone.

239381.explanation: Poverty has many faces. That is why it is important to set up poverty schemes broadly. So that those who need them can also make use of them.

239382.explanation: The government is often one of the biggest creditors when people can no longer pay their bills. Instead of fines, the municipality should help these people to stand on their own feet again. That is why it is good that the municipality prioritizes perspective over fines.

239383.explanation: Ede is growing, which means more residents travel from A to B by bike, public transport, walking, or car. With more residents, the number of parking spaces cannot decrease.

239385.explanation: Being able to travel safely from A to B is very important to D66 Ede. For both cars and cyclists, it is safer to separate bicycles and cars as much as possible. Where that is not possible, we choose a 30 KM/h speed limit. This way every child can safely bike to school!

239386.explanation: Discrimination and racism do not belong in Ede. Everyone must be treated equally. When this does not happen, the municipality should be ready to help you. That is why it is good if the municipality employs people knowledgeable about combating discrimination and racism.

239387.explanation: We strongly encourage civil servants to do internships or otherwise be actively involved in the Ede community. However, we do not think civil servants should be forced to do so.

239389.explanation: Culture is for everyone. We all enjoy music, film, series, dance, or other forms of art and culture. That is why the municipality should continue to invest in culture and ensure everyone can participate. The Frisokazerne is such a place where that is possible.

239391.explanation: When people move to Ede, we want them to participate as soon as possible in all the wonderful associations our municipality has to offer. Therefore, we want to help new residents join an association.

239392.explanation: Everyone enjoys music, dance, films, series, or art. It brings people together and serves as a creative outlet for many to express themselves. That is why it is important that the municipality of Ede continues to invest in culture.

239393.explanation: It is important that everyone can be themselves. Therefore, it would help if sports clubs also demonstrate that you are welcome regardless of your sexual orientation. We are happy to have that conversation with sports clubs but will not impose any obligations.

239394.explanation: We thoroughly enjoy the wonderful events at the open-air theater. But that must go hand in hand with good agreements with local residents that are respected. That is currently not the case. Before the municipality dreams of more, it must first honor the current agreements.

239397.explanation: D66 Ede opposes yo-yo policies and wants clarity for both local residents and asylum seekers. Opening and then quickly closing a shelter does not contribute to that. However, we want there to always be a set date to review the future of the shelter.

239398.explanation: All municipalities must do what is necessary to create sufficient shelter. If all municipalities comply with the distribution law, there would be no shelter crisis.

239399.explanation: Asylum seekers must integrate and participate from day one. If a shelter is located far outside inhabited areas, integration is difficult. Therefore, we want the planning of new neighborhoods to consider the proper integration of small-scale shelters.

239400.explanation: Farmers deserve a fair wage for their hard work. This must go hand in hand with less emissions and better animal welfare. Therefore, the municipality must collaborate with the province and national government to support farmers in the transition to lower emissions, fewer animals, and a fair wage.

239401.explanation: Our peace and security are our greatest asset. That is why all municipalities must contribute to housing defense. Ede will also have to make a fair contribution.

239402.explanation: A green neighborhood with space to play outside and walk is a neighborhood where people like to live. Sometimes a parking spot must give way or be relocated to achieve this. This way, we ensure a beautiful, livable neighborhood with sufficient parking.

239403.explanation: The return of the wolf to the Netherlands is a good sign for Dutch nature. At the same time, we must learn to live with the wolf and take effective measures to facilitate that. The province is leading in this matter.

239404.explanation: Ede is a municipality where we enjoy nature to the fullest. We must continue doing this in moderation to strengthen the quality of our nature. Permanently closing large parts of nature does not contribute to this, according to D66 Ede.

239405.explanation: Our safety is a great asset. It is important that our Ede police have enough cells available to allow people to cool down and/or to protect them (from themselves).

239406.explanation: Violence against police and enforcement officers (BOAs) is increasing. Therefore, we must protect our men and women who stand up for our safety every day as best as possible. In exceptional cases, small cameras may be used to ensure safety, with privacy playing an important role.

239407.explanation: The police employ highly skilled men and women who can make the best assessments themselves. The use of police dogs should be limited as much as possible but not banned.

239408.explanation: Everyone should feel safe on the street. This starts with tackling and apprehending those who misbehave. The municipality must enforce rules and guarantee a safe public space by listening to women, not by teaching women to fight.

239409.explanation: The average waiting time for a social rental home is currently 4 years and is increasing every year. If we want young people in Ede, Bennekom, Lunteren, Otterlo, or one of our other villages to find an affordable home, we must keep building while preserving the village character.

239410.explanation: If the market does not solve that many residents of Ede cannot find an affordable home, the municipality should consider doing so itself. Together, we can ensure that the waiting time for a social rental home no longer lasts 4 years.

239412.explanation: Houses are meant to be lived in, not to remain empty. If residents have to wait 4 years for an affordable home, it is inexplicable why others have homes standing empty for years. That is why we want to impose an extra tax on such properties.

239413.explanation: An extra floor or an additional home next to an existing one can help reduce pressure on the housing market. We should focus on solving the housing shortage instead of imposing more rules about what is allowed in certain parts of Ede.

239415.explanation: Sufficient exercise and sports are good for everyone. Building the habit of doing this enough starts when you are young. That is why we must continue to encourage young people to play sports and get moving.

239416.explanation: People with lower incomes also love their pets, but vet bills can sometimes be higher than expected. The municipality can already assist people through special social assistance.

239417.explanation: A good breakfast is crucial to enable every child to enjoy and learn at school. Unfortunately, there are still children who go to school without breakfast. For these children, the school should be able to arrange breakfast.

239418.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve clarity about the rules that apply in the city center. Residents deserve clean air. Instead of constantly changing the rules, the municipality must keep its promises.

239419.explanation: Homelessness is primarily a housing problem. Therefore, the municipality must (where possible) offer housing to people who are homeless or at risk of becoming so. This is better for these people and better for Ede.

239420.explanation: Keeping a close watch on elderly people helps prevent illnesses, ailments, and loneliness. By sending a welfare worker to people from age 75 onwards, help can be implemented earlier if needed.

239421.explanation: Relationships are a private matter according to D66 Ede. Sometimes it is better to separate amicably than to argue by staying together. The municipality should not interfere with that.

239363.explanation: We save reserves for a specific purpose, for example, the development of urgently needed affordable housing. Using saved reserves for tax reductions not only endangers the construction of extra homes but also makes the municipality's financial position less secure.

239364.explanation: The CDA wants to use the proceeds from the tourist tax partly to improve walking, cycling, and equestrian routes. Therefore, we do not want to spend all proceeds on maintaining nature reserves but also on facilities for tourists outside those areas.

239365.explanation: The CDA believes that dog tax is outdated. Dog owners are unfairly charged for the costs of public space compared to other pet owners. However, we want the revenues from dog tax to be offset against other municipal taxes.

239366.explanation: The CDA wants active advocacy to attract innovative, sustainable, and knowledge-intensive businesses to Ede. This stimulates employment in our municipality and helps build a fair economy that works for people.

239367.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain current rules on Sunday openings. In the town of Ede, we want shops to be allowed to open on Sundays; in the villages, they should not. Shops should remain closed on Christmas Day, according to the CDA.

239368.explanation: Ede's identity is characterized by beautiful nature and the agricultural sector. Our municipality already has relatively many nature reserves. The CDA therefore wants to keep agricultural land available for farming as much as possible to offer perspective to our agricultural sector.

239369.explanation: The CDA believes that the treatment and return of asylum seekers who have exhausted their applications is a task for the national government and the Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND). We therefore believe the municipality has no task in sheltering such asylum seekers and oppose this statement.

239370.explanation: The housing shortage is not solved by no longer giving priority to people with residence permits for social rental homes. The CDA therefore chooses to build extra social rental homes. This approach aims to reduce waiting lists and create more opportunities for starters and seniors.

239371.explanation: The CDA opts for maximum solar energy on roofs and no solar parks on agricultural land. We support smart combinations with energy storage and collective sharing of energy.

239372.explanation: The CDA wants to work together with entrepreneurs and owners to include as many existing toilets as possible in the Hoge Nood app. This way, we utilize the existing strengths of society and make toilets accessible to everyone.

239373.explanation: The CDA chooses to expand events. Ede is a growing municipality, and the range of events should grow accordingly. We want to involve the interests of residents, local neighbors, and organizations.

239374.explanation: Ede will continue to grow in the coming years, which may increase urban issues. This is a risk for safety. The CDA advocates for a safe and livable Ede and therefore wants the capacity of municipal enforcers to grow alongside the municipality. We support this statement.

239375.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of cannabis sales. However, maintaining the current two coffeeshops allows some supervision of the currently inevitable cannabis trade. The CDA believes this minimizes negative consequences as much as possible and remains opposed to opening new coffeeshops.

239376.explanation: The CDA chooses to tackle nuisance caused by fatbikes. We want to do this by studying and adopting successful examples from other municipalities.

239377.explanation: The CDA opposes raising this threshold. We believe this drives up prices in an already overheated housing market, which will not help first-time buyers. We choose to make housing accessible for starters by building a new street in every village and going vertical in the city.

239378.explanation: The CDA prioritizes residents where possible. Whether young or old, we want all residents of our municipality to live near their existing communities and shared facilities.

239379.explanation: The CDA chooses 30% social housing in new construction projects, with the possibility to compensate between projects. This target is not achievable everywhere.

239380.explanation: To address the housing shortage, we want to build 15,000 homes by 2040. For this, the CDA supports high-rise buildings in the town of Ede: up to 70 meters high around the station, Stadspoort, Bellestein, and Ede-Zuid. Building upwards allows us to preserve more green spaces and nature.

239381.explanation: Currently, many people do not make use of existing poverty schemes. The CDA wants as many eligible people as possible to actually use these schemes. We want to focus on this.

239382.explanation: The CDA supports debt assumption by the municipality in extreme cases. We want to create a clear situation where a family has only one creditor.

239383.explanation: The CDA chooses to keep parking in order: sufficient parking space for residents and businesses. We want appropriate parking standards: lower standards in the denser city center and suitable standards in the villages.

239385.explanation: The CDA chooses 30 km/h as the speed limit in built-up areas where bicycles share the road with cars. We want to separate bicycle traffic from car traffic as much as possible. Where this is not possible, we want to reduce speed to 30 km/h. Together, this ensures a safer Ede.

239386.explanation: Discrimination reports are already being tracked. Unfortunately, many people experience discrimination. The CDA therefore believes action is necessary. However, adding an extra municipal coordinator just to keep track will not change anything.

239387.explanation: We find a civic internship for civil servants unnecessary. Many municipal employees come from Ede themselves and have sufficient ties. However, the CDA supports a civic service period for youth, so they get to know ways to contribute to society.

239389.explanation: The CDA finds it important to have places where art and culture can flourish. The location near the station is a beautiful, central, and accessible place for this. We do think it is important that initiatives within the cultural center finance themselves as much as possible.

239391.explanation: The CDA chooses a warm welcome for new residents of Ede. Every new resident of the municipality receives a trial membership for a club of their choice to immediately participate in Ede's community life. Together we build a strong community.

239392.explanation: Currently, the municipality of Ede spends almost the least on art and culture per inhabitant among the 50 largest municipalities in the country. The CDA therefore believes that Ede should spend more rather than less on art and culture.

239393.explanation: The CDA supports hanging the LGBTIQ+ flag on municipal buildings on Coming Out Day. We believe hanging the flag at sports clubs is a choice for the clubs themselves.

239394.explanation: The CDA opts for expanding events. Ede is a growing municipality and the event offerings must grow accordingly, for example at the Open Air Theatre. We want to involve the interests of residents, neighbors, and organizations and uphold agreements made.

239397.explanation: The CDA favors a reliable government and wants to provide long-term clarity about the duration of reception locations. We prefer long-term reception facilities to expensive short-term solutions where asylum seekers must repeatedly move from location to location.

239398.explanation: The CDA supports decent reception of asylum seekers by following the Distribution Act. This means we want to adhere to the legally required number of asylum seekers to be housed. This is already a big task. We therefore find accommodating more asylum seekers unfeasible.

239399.explanation: The CDA supports small-scale reception locations near residential areas and amenities such as supermarkets. This can encourage quick integration and limit possible nuisance for neighbors and nearby businesses. The edges of residential areas are suitable locations.

239400.explanation: Many farmers in Ede are already stopping. The CDA fears we will end up with too few farmers. Therefore, the CDA wants to create space for agricultural entrepreneurs who want to continue. We want to provide them with long-term clarity in their role as food producers and landscape managers.

239401.explanation: The CDA finds military exercises essential for protecting our national security, especially in these geopolitically uncertain times. We want to give the Defense Ministry room to conduct extra exercises in the designated areas in Ede.

239402.explanation: If we want to make space in the city for housing, green areas, amenities, and meeting places, lowering the parking standard belongs there too. Where we densify, the CDA would like to build parking garages: this way we can build more homes, create more green space, and retain parking spots at the same time.

239403.explanation: Not only wildlife is on the menu: we see an increase in attacks on livestock, farm animals, and interactions between wolves and humans. The CDA therefore supports managing the wolf population like other wildlife to prevent nuisance.

239404.explanation: The CDA supports a good balance between nature and recreation. The province of Gelderland has made the Veluwe recreation zoning plan for this. We want to keep and implement the agreed zoning. Therefore, the CDA is against closing off even more nature areas.

239405.explanation: The CDA chooses an effective police force that fits the size of Ede. Therefore, we advocate the return of the cell complex at the Ede police station. Currently, detainees must be taken to Elst, which costs much-needed police presence on the streets in Ede.

239406.explanation: The CDA supports extra support for our municipal enforcement officers. We want to equip Ede’s municipal officers (BOAs) with bodycams so they can work safely and decisively. The CDA also wants BOAs to keep their batons. This way, we keep Ede safe.

239407.explanation: The CDA stands for an effective police force that matches the size of Ede. The use of police dogs is sometimes necessary. The CDA is very pleased that the police have these dogs available to maintain order, as recently during the riots in Huygenspark.

239408.explanation: The CDA believes the safety of girls and women deserves more attention. We want, for example, safer public spaces and tackling unsafe places. The CDA does not consider paying for self-defense training for women a suitable solution nor a municipal role.

239409.explanation: Currently, thousands of people are looking for housing: young people delay starting a family and people have been on waiting lists for years. Therefore, the CDA wants to build 15,000 homes by 2040. We opt for growth opportunities in all villages: adding a street or neighborhood in every village.

239410.explanation: To tackle the housing shortage, we want to build 15,000 homes by 2040. However, the municipality building homes itself does not solve the housing shortage. The municipality is not a construction company but a government that stimulates contractors and property owners to build.

239412.explanation: The CDA supports an active approach to vacancy. We do not want empty spaces left unused in a time of major housing shortage. Therefore, we support introducing a municipal vacancy levy.

239413.explanation: To address the housing shortage, we want to build 15,000 homes by 2040. For this, the CDA supports high-rise buildings in multiple locations in Ede: up to 70 meters high around the station, center, Stadspoort, Bellestein, and Ede-Zuid. Building upwards allows us to preserve more green spaces and nature.

239415.explanation: The CDA wants to make sports accessible for everyone. We want to expand the existing Sport Card Ede for students at the Knowledge Campus to all young people living in our municipality. We do not find it necessary to make this discount card completely free (currently €16 per month).

239416.explanation: Currently, many people do not make use of existing poverty schemes. The CDA wants as many eligible people as possible to actually use these schemes. We want to focus on this instead of a new scheme for veterinary costs.

239417.explanation: The CDA supports a free school breakfast for children for whom this is not obvious at home. Therefore, we support the existing national School Meals Program. We want schools to keep the choice to register for it themselves. The CDA finds additional municipal funding unnecessary.

239418.explanation: The CDA wants to introduce a zero-emission zone only if it is feasible and affordable for entrepreneurs. We also want the municipality to adopt a realistic pace and provide appropriate support to entrepreneurs. We do not consider introduction on January 1, 2030 realistic now.

239419.explanation: The CDA chooses Housing First as a springboard for people who are long-term homeless and benefit from a stable home without prior conditions. At the same time, we want these people to receive intensive support in their further recovery.

239420.explanation: The CDA supports fighting loneliness and accessible support for the elderly. We therefore want welfare workers to visit from age 75. However, the CDA chooses 'own strength first': professional help is only deployed if the personal network cannot provide it.

239421.explanation: The family is the cornerstone of society: we want it to be a safe, warm place where children can grow up. When a family has complex problems, we choose coordinated care and one clear plan. We believe family coaches can make a big difference in this.

239363.explanation: The municipality of Ede has consistently had budget surpluses in recent years. Citizen Interests has repeatedly made realistic proposals to return these surpluses to our residents. The coalition parties have repeatedly blocked this.

239364.explanation: Tourist tax is not only intended to maintain nature. It also pays for other costs, such as construction and maintenance of roads, parking lots, bike and walking paths, signage for tourist routes, traffic measures during concerts, etc.

239365.explanation: BurgerBelangen has been advocating for years to abolish the medieval dog tax and continues to fight for this. It is unfair that dog owners have to pay tax while owners of other pets do not. More and more municipalities have rightly abolished this tax.

239366.explanation: Ede is already an attractive location for businesses. Investing additional resources will only cause the business parks to fill up faster and make it harder for local companies to expand in their own town. Our space is not unlimited.

239367.explanation: BurgerBelangen supports Sunday openings. Not because it has to be, but because it may be. The choice of whether a store opens on Sunday should be left to the entrepreneur. This freedom of choice should not be restricted by municipal regulations.

239368.explanation: Ede has previously chosen to profile itself as Foodvalley. This includes an environment where agriculture flourishes. However, Ede must also comply with nature protection laws. This means it is not excluded that we may have to sacrifice agricultural land to comply with the law.

239369.explanation: BurgerBelangen does not want the municipality to provide shelter or long-term support to rejected asylum seekers. BurgerBelangen believes asylum seekers should be sheltered in their own region and therefore does not want asylum seeker centers in the municipality of Ede.

239370.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit must join the waiting list for social rental housing like everyone else. It is unfair to give this group priority. BurgerBelangen therefore wants to abolish this priority to eliminate division.

239371.explanation: BurgerBelangen believes solar panels do not belong on agricultural land, but a farmer should – like anyone else – have the opportunity to place a limited number of solar panels for personal use.

239372.explanation: There are insufficient public toilets, and it would be beneficial to add several at places where many people gather.

239373.explanation: BurgerBelangen believes Ede has an appropriate number of events that partly shape the character of Ede. It is important that the rules are followed and that enforcement occurs to prevent nuisance.

239374.explanation: The enforcement department is especially difficult to reach in the evenings and this must improve. Enforcement is often insufficient in case of nuisance. We believe current capacity must be better utilized, and extra enforcement officers should be added if necessary.

239375.explanation: BurgerBelangen wants no tolerance policy but clear rules and regulated policy only for existing coffeeshops. A ban is not a solution. Illegal sales will increase instead. It is naive to think that if coffeeshops disappear, the market and demand will also disappear.

239376.explanation: BurgerBelangen notes there is often nuisance and dangerous driving behavior by youths on modified fatbikes. It is important that more frequent checks and fines are issued. Fortunately, there are also youths who ride their fatbikes properly and legally.

239377.explanation: Houses are becoming more expensive and BurgerBelangen wants to help first-time buyers where realistic. Research shows that with the current limit, first-time buyers can still be helped. BurgerBelangen believes this limit should increase along with house prices.

239378.explanation: BurgerBelangen wants to go even further. BurgerBelangen believes residents of villages should have priority for social rental housing in their own village.

239379.explanation: BurgerBelangen is satisfied with the current percentage of 30% and favors diverse neighborhoods consisting of different target groups. This prevents disadvantaged neighborhoods and keeps neighborhoods livable. Additionally, diverse neighborhoods are financially easier to realize for construction companies.

239380.explanation: BurgerBelangen does not want massive high-rise buildings. Flats taller than usual should fit the location, such as in Ede center and other suitable locations like the station area. The plans are too focused on the national housing shortage and not on building for our own residents.

239381.explanation: BurgerBelangen finds it important that those entitled can actually make use of the arrangements. The 130% limit used in Ede is generous, but we keep an eye on those just outside this limit to explore possibilities for tailored solutions if necessary.

239382.explanation: People who are entitled qualify if they meet the set conditions. Obviously, misuse must be prevented. Ending this arrangement would severely disadvantage too many people.

239383.explanation: The number of people owning cars continues to increase, and research shows this will continue in coming years. Therefore, it is illogical to reduce the number of parking spaces. A shortage of parking spaces is actually emerging.

239385.explanation: BurgerBelangen finds applying a 30 km/h speed limit on all these roads going too far. However, we support separating bicycle and car traffic wherever possible to make cycling as safe as possible. It is important that traffic flow remains fast and smooth.

239386.explanation: The municipality of Ede uses the national discrimination reporting center and receives an annual report on reports in our municipality. A separate coordinator for this is therefore unnecessary.
